title: corylus avellana f. contorta
intro: Corylus avellana f. contorta, also known as contorted hazel, is a variant of the common hazel tree (Corylus avellana) characterized by its unique, twisted or contorted branches. The bark, leaves, and seeds of this plant have been used in traditional herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of contorted hazel include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant support and potential protection against certain cancers. Contorted hazel can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy for various health concerns.
